business.headline: Sensex opens 300 points higher, Nifty above 24,100; IndiGo up 3%Sensex opens 300 points higher, Nifty above 24,100; IndiGo up 3%

RBI says banks must notify pensioners before recovering excess pensionThe RBI has told banks to notify government pensioners before recovering any excess pension paid into their accounts. The amendment seeks to stop sudden debits and set a clearer recovery process through notice and consent.

Lost money to online fraud? RBI's new rule might just save youThe RBI has set new rules on who bears losses from fraudulent electronic banking transactions. Customers who report quickly can get full reversals or capped compensation under a time-bound process.

Stock mismatch, mega offshore deals: What ED found during raids on Rajesh ExportsThe Enforcement Directorate searched nine premises linked to Rajesh Exports in Bengaluru and Mumbai under FEMA. The agency says missing records, stock gaps, trade set-offs and suspicious trades are now under investigation.

SK Hynix surpasses Samsung as South Korea's most valuable company amid AI chip boomSK Hynix has emerged as one of the biggest beneficiaries thanks to its leadership in high-bandwidth memory (HBM) chips, a key component used in advanced AI systems.

Elon Musk no longer a trillionaire as SpaceX, Tesla shares declineThe decline in Musk's fortune came after investors sold technology stocks amid concerns about an artificial intelligence-driven market bubble and the possibility of higher interest rates.

From inspections to intelligent compliance: The next step in implementing the ESI ActIn the final part of this series on labour laws and Inspector Raj, this article examines why expanding social-security coverage is only half the battle, and how risk-based inspections, data analytics and AI-driven compliance could help ensure that workers actually receive the protections guaranteed under the ESI Act.

Why the Calcutta Stock Exchange has been shut for over a decadeIndia's oldest regional stock exchange has not seen a single trade since 2013. With West Bengal now proposing to revive it, here is exactly why it shut down in the first place.

Sensex ends 790 points higher, Nifty tops 24,000; IndiGo up 5%The S&P BSE Sensex climbed 790.54 points, or 1.04%, to close at 76,991.22, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 197.55 points, or 0.83%, to settle at 24,021.65.

What is WhatsApp's new chief Kunal Shah's net worth?From launching CRED to building one of India's best-known fintech firms, Kunal Shah has come a long way. But how much wealth has he accumulated along the journey?

From HRA to NPS: 6 reasons old tax regime still helps you save more taxThe old tax regime may no longer be the default option, but it still offers several powerful tax-saving benefits. Could these six deductions help lower your tax bill more than the new regime?

CRED built a premium user base. Is that why Meta is paying $900 million?Meta is set to invest $900 million in CRED for a reported 20% stake. The deal points to the value of CRED's affluent users as much as its fintech stack.

How Noida's new airport redefines Uttar Pradesh's economic geographyThe start of flights at Noida International Airport marks a new phase for Jewar in UP. Its cargo-led, multi-modal design could reshape exports, industry and regional connectivity across North India.

Did India's compliance push cost it the next investment boom?India has improved ease of doing business over the past decade, but is that enough in a world increasingly shaped by AI, semiconductors and deep technology?

RBI not preparing for interest rate hikes yet, Governor Malhotra clarifiesMalhotra said the RBI is keeping an eye on whether higher oil prices start affecting the prices of other goods and services across the economy. According to him, such broader inflationary pressure has not appeared so far.

Sensex jumps nearly 800 points: Why is stock market rising today?Stock markets rebounded sharply on Wednesday as banking and IT shares led gains after the previous session's sell-off. Lower crude prices and supportive RBI signals lifted sentiment, even as monsoon and US rate risks remained.

Adani AGM: India's future cannot wait, says Gautam Adani on growth roadmapSpeaking at the Adani AGM, Chairman Gautam Adani said the group remained focused on nation-building despite facing challenges over the past year.

IRFC shares fall 5% today. Here's why the railway PSU stock is under pressureIRFC share price: The stock was trading at Rs 93.46 around 9:49 am, down over 5% for the day. The decline adds to the pressure on the PSU stock, which has fallen nearly 7% over the past month, 23% in six months and more than 32% over the past year.

Markets open flat as crude slump offsets hawkish Fed concernsBenchmark indices opened cautiously on Wednesday, with lower crude prices and a rebound in IT stocks aiding stability after the previous session's sell-off. Investors remained watchful of the monsoon, foreign flows and the US Federal Reserve as mixed cues kept sentiment restrained.

8th Pay Commission: OPS demand pending, but govt gives relief to these employeesThe government has said compassionate appointment cases filed by December 31, 2003, can be considered under the old pension rules even if the appointment came later. The clarification offers relief to affected employees while the wider OPS debate continues before the 8th Pay Commission.

entertainment.headline: Priyanka Chopra on transitioning from Bollywood to Hollywood: Haven't done much Actor Priyanka Chopra Jonas said Hollywood has not yet offered her the same range of roles she enjoyed in Bollywood. The actor also opened up about producing her own films, balancing motherhood and work and her future career goals.

Ram Charan spends time with uncle Pawan Kalyan, praises Jana Sena Party office Ram Charan met Pawan Kalyan in Amaravati and shared photographs from the visit online. The post drew fan attention while highlighting progress at the new Jana Sena Party office.

Vishal Bhardwaj, costume designer Eka Lakhani and more set to become Oscar voters Haider filmmaker Vishal Bhardwaj and Rocky Aur Rani costume designer Eka Lakhani are among the Indian film professionals invited to join the Academy. Here's what the honour could mean for the Oscars.

Explained: Why Tom Cruise's 3-minute Digger trailer only has 41-second film footage Tom Cruise's first trailer for Digger is intriguing. It hides the actor's face. Interestingly, the over-3-minute clip has 41-second footage from the film. What's the trailer trying to say?

Exclusive: Panchayat's Binod shares inside story of PM Modi meet and his viral post Panchayat actor Ashok Pathak recently met Prime Minister Narendra Modi at an event in New Delhi, where the PM greeted him with a playful reference to his popular character, Binod. In an exclusive conversation, the actor recalled their interaction and revealed what Modi said to him.

Taylor Swift's Love Story performance comes with perfect ring moment. Watch Pop star Taylor Swift surprised fans by performing one of her iconic songs, Love Story, at her fiance and NFL player Travis Kelce's Tight End University in Nashville. She also flashed her engagement ring while singing the song's iconic proposal lyric.

Gigi and Bella Hadid's mother Yolanda gets engaged. Here's what we know Former model and television personality Yolanda Hadid is reportedly engaged to real estate developer Randy Kendrick after a largely private relationship. The development comes more than a year after her split from construction executive Joseph Jingoli.

Passport not proof of citizenship? Javed Akhtar questions ministry's 'absurd' move Writer-lyricist Javed Akhtar on Wednesday criticised the Ministry of External Affairs' statement that a passport is not proof of citizenship. The remark sparked a wider debate on what counts as conclusive citizenship evidence.

Gujarati stalwart Arvind Vekaria, father of Taarak Mehta actor Tanmay, dies Veteran Gujarati actor and director Arvind Vekaria, father of Tarak Mehta Ka Ooltah Chashmah Tanmay Vekaria, died on Wednesday. CINTAA confirmed the news and paid tribute to the stalwart on social media.

Ranbir, Yash amp up Ramayana buzz at fan meet; Kunal Kapoor's Indra Look unveiled Ranbir Kapoor and Yash attended a Ramayana event for more than 150 content creators. Exclusive BTS footage and attendee posts have deepened curiosity around the film's scale and vision.

Samantha confirms pregnancy at Maa Inti Bangaaram event, to go on maternity leave Actor Samantha confirmed that she and husband Raj Nidimoru are expecting their first child. The actor announced the news at Maa Inti Bangaaram success meet in Hyderabad.

Priyanka Chopra says household chores are basic adulting, not women's work Actor Priyanka Chopra challenged the idea that cooking and cleaning are women's work. Her remarks framed household chores as basic life skills rather than gendered responsibilities.

Imtiaz Ali reacts to calls for Main Vaapas Aaunga to be tax-free in Punjab Calls to make Main Vaapas Aaunga tax-free in Punjab have found support from director Imtiaz Ali. The filmmaker said the demand is worth considering and that the team would be looking into the possibility.

Mammootty breaks silence on film body crisis after Shwetha Menon's resignation Days after AMMA's executive committee resigned, actor Mammootty has finally addressed the controversy. The actor described the ongoing crisis as a 'small matter' and said all issues would eventually be resolved.

Raakh's 'Babu' Akash Makhija says fans said his eyes reminded them of Irrfan Akash Makhija has spoken about the response to his performance as Babu in Raakh. He said the role ended years of waiting while also bringing intense reactions and fresh hope for work.

Bobby Deol is clueless about Latent 2, dodges question on Samay Raina's show A viral clip shows actor Bobby Deol responding to a question about India's Got Latent Season 2 during Alpha promotions. He reacted to actors Alia Bhatt and Sharvari's appearance on the show.

Alka Yagnik seen in wheelchair after Padma honour, video sparks concern A video of Alka Yagnik being assisted in a wheelchair surfaced online after her Padma Bhushan appearance. The clip drew concern from fans as the singer spoke about her health struggles and gradual return.
The Devil Wears Prada 2 OTT: When and where to watch the chic fashion-drama The doors of Runway Magazine are opening once again. The Devil Wears Prada 2 is finally making its way to OTT.

Vijay Deverakonda-Arnold Vosloo's face-off is Ranabaali's biggest moment: Director Director Rahul Sankrityan revealed that Ranabaali's climactic showdown between Vijay Deverakonda and The Mummy star Arnold Vosloo took 16 days to shoot and is a major highlight of the film.

general news.headline: Twin Venezuela quakes leave cities in ruins, high casualties feared Live UpdatesSurvivors described scenes "like a horror movie" after twin earthquakes -- the most powerful to strike Venezuela in over a century -- devastated parts of the Latin American country. Buildings collapsed, families searched through rubble for loved ones, and rescuers worked through the night amid fears of high casualties.

Not mentally prepared for marriage: Siya's new motive emerges in Pune murder caseThe initial police probe found that Ketan Agrawal's fiancee, Siya Goyal, was allegedly not mentally prepared for marriage and was under family pressure to go ahead with the wedding. Investigators allege she later conspired with her alleged lover to kill Ketan and pass off his death as an accident.

Pak imposes blockade on POK, tries to starve it of food, medicine to quell protestsAs food, fuel and medicine shortages worsen across POK, residents and opposition parties are accusing Islamabad of restricting the flow of essential goods into the region. Pakistani officials have denied the allegations, but reports suggest authorities have disrupted supplies to quell the growing protest movement in the illegally-occupied territory.

Passport is a travel document, not document of citizenship: GovtThe Ministry of External Affairs said a passport is not a document that establishes citizenship, even though it is issued only to Indian citizens. The clarification, made on Passport Seva Divas, has renewed debate over what counts as conclusive proof of citizenship.

Pak threatened to wipe out Israel over Mossad plot to kill Munir: Brazilian journoBrazilian journalist and geopolitical expert Pepe Escobar alleged that Israeli intelligence agency Mossad plotted to eliminate Pakistani military chief Asim Munir and the country's delegation in Geneva during Iran talks. He said Pakistan, after getting to know of the plot, threatened to "wipe Israel off the map". Escobar's claims were rejected by Pakistani journalists.

NYT analysed Usha Vance's $8.75 maternity dress. This is her responseUsha Vance mocked a New York Times reading of her discounted maternity dress as political messaging. Her post and Old Navy receipt then fuelled a wider online debate over fashion, class and everyday shopping.

After 51 years, Emergency enters NCERT's Class 9 textbooksFor the first time, NCERT has included the 1975–77 Emergency in the Class 9 Social Science syllabus as part of the NEP 2020 curriculum overhaul. The move comes as India marks 51 years since the Emergency was imposed, introducing this pivotal chapter of Indian democracy to Class 9 students.

RBI wants to mandate AI kill switch for all banks, human oversight for all decisionsThe Reserve Bank of India is planning to bring new rules for banks and financial institutions that use AI. As per the latest draft guidelines, the RBI wants to mandate a kill-switch that would override or stop any AI model being used within a bank's systems.

981 days in injury exile, Neymar brought to tears on Brazil returnBrazil samba-ed their way into the knockouts of the FIFA World Cup with a statement 3-0 win against Scotland. Neymar Jr. walked into the pitch for the national team after an injury-exile of 981 days.

How Ketan's sister's doubts exposed Pune murder plot by Siya and ChetanWhat began as a reported trekking accident took a dramatic turn after Ketan Agrawal's sister questioned his fiancee. Investigators now claim the fatal fall was preceded by multiple failed attempts and a carefully planned conspiracy.

Grew up in a stable Hindu home: Usha Vance on why she didn't convert like JD VanceMonths after JD Vance reflected on finding stability through faith, Usha Vance reveals why she never felt the need to convert to Christianity and how their interfaith marriage works.

Venezuela's once-in-a-century quake: Why two earthquakes hit togetherVenezuela witnessed a massive disaster, but there's more to it. Here's what an earthquake doublet is, why Venezuela is vulnerable to major quakes, and what could happen next.

R Madhavan wore a Titan watch to the Padma Shri ceremony. You can't guess it's priceR Madhavan received the Padma Shri at Rashtrapati Bhavan wearing Titan's rare Jalsa watch, and it was more than just a timepiece. The story hit home too.

We want death penalty: Mumbai local murder victim's family seeks justiceMayank Lohar, a 22-year-old Mumbai man, was fatally stabbed on local train by a 30-year-old accused during a dispute over keeping the train doors open in heavy rain.

Europe bakes under deadly 44°C heat dome; death toll mounts, schools shutA rare heat dome has pushed temperatures across Europe to dangerous highs, disrupting travel, schools and tourism. The extreme spell has turned deadly and is straining farms, power supplies and public health systems.

Kolkata warehouse collapse: Engineer, supervisor arrested; owner among 4 detainedKolkata Police have arrested a structural engineer and a site supervisor after the Taratala warehouse collapse that killed five workers. The SIT probe will examine the design, materials, construction process and project approvals.

Explained: Why Tom Cruise's 3-minute Digger trailer only has 41-second film footageTom Cruise's first trailer for Digger is intriguing. It hides the actor's face. Interestingly, the over-3-minute clip has 41-second footage from the film. What's the trailer trying to say?

Real alien s**t: What are Iran's 'jellyfish' drones, likely behind US jet crash?A US F-15E Strike Eagle pilot claims a jellyfish-like drone swarm may have brought down the fighter during the Iran conflict — a development that, if verified, could signal a significant evolution in Iran's drone warfare capabilities.

Five people drown in Cauvery river in Karnataka, four victims from same familyThe tragedy occurred when one of the victims slipped into the deeper section of the water. In an attempt to save her, several members of her family entered the water and drowned.

Brace for rain on Thursday: Monsoon pushes, IMD warns of heavy showersThe southwest monsoon advanced further into western and central India on Wednesday, with the IMD forecasting heavy rainfall across multiple regions.

I am a doctor, and I tell patients not to celebrate when dengue fever dropsMost people think dengue is only about fever and low platelets, but the biggest danger often begins when the fever starts coming down. As an internal medicine specialist, I want everyone to know what truly matters during dengue treatment.

Lost money to online fraud? RBI's new rule might just save youThe RBI has set new rules on who bears losses from fraudulent electronic banking transactions. Customers who report quickly can get full reversals or capped compensation under a time-bound process.

Exclusive: Panchayat's Binod shares inside story of PM Modi meet and his viral postPanchayat actor Ashok Pathak recently met Prime Minister Narendra Modi at an event in New Delhi, where the PM greeted him with a playful reference to his popular character, Binod. In an exclusive conversation, the actor recalled their interaction and revealed what Modi said to him.

Anand Mahindra moved by 80-year-old violinist playing on Kolkata streets, offers helpFor years, Bhagwan Mallick has played the violin on the streets of Kolkata to earn a living. Now, the 80-year-old musician's story has caught the attention of industrialist Anand Mahindra, who has offered to help.

sports.headline: Wyatt-Hodge helps England reach semis with West Indies win Danni Wyatt-Hodge drove England to a 38-run win over West Indies at Lord's and into the semi-finals. The result avenged England's 2024 group-stage loss and left West Indies needing to beat Ireland to qualify.

981 days in injury exile, Neymar brought to tears on Brazil return Brazil samba-ed their way into the knockouts of the FIFA World Cup with a statement 3-0 win against Scotland. Neymar Jr. walked into the pitch for the national team after an injury-exile of 981 days.

T20 World Cup: Bruised India face tricky Bangladesh test in must-win game Women's T20 World Cup, IND vs BAN: India meet Bangladesh in a must-win Women's T20 World Cup match at Old Trafford after defeat to South Africa. The result could turn on India's fragile middle order, Bangladesh's spinners and the pressure of qualification.

Morocco overcomes Haiti scare to enter WC 2026 knockouts as Group C runners-up Morocco beat Haiti 4-2 in an instant World Cup classic to finish second in Group C. In a match that saw four goals in the first half, the Atlas Lions were able to battle on score two in the final 45 minutes to get the victory.

Neymar's return, Vini Jr's night: Brazil head to knockouts with 3-0 win vs Scotland SCO vs BRA, FIFA World Cup 2026: Vinicius Jr scored twice as Brazil beat Scotland 3-0 to top Group C, while Neymar made his long-awaited return from injury in front of a packed Miami Stadium to cap a memorable night.

KOR 0-1 RSA Live: South Africa achieve history, qualify for knockouts South Korea vs South Africa, FIFA World Cup 2026 Live: South Africa have achieved a historic qualification to the knockout round, courtesy of Maseko's second half goal against South Korea. Meanwhile, Mexico stay perfect in the group stage and hammer Czechia 3-0 in their final group stage game.

Switzerland deny Canada top spot, but hosts seal historic World Cup knockout berth SUI vs CAN, FIFA World Cup 2026: Canada's World Cup dream rolls on despite a 2-1 defeat to Switzerland, with the co-hosts reaching the knockout stage for the first time in history as the Swiss clinched top spot in Group B.

Bosnia knock Qatar out of World Cup with 3-1 win to keep knockout hopes alive Bosnia and Herzegovina beat Qatar 3-1 in Seattle to keep their World Cup campaign alive. The result knocked Qatar out and left Bosnia relying on other groups to reach the Round of 32 for the first time in their history.

Switzerland deny Canada top spot, but hosts seal historic World Cup knockout berth SUI vs CAN, FIFA World Cup 2026: Canada's World Cup dream rolls on despite a 2-1 defeat to Switzerland, with the co-hosts reaching the knockout stage for the first time in history as the Swiss clinched top spot in Group B.

SCO vs BRA Highlights: Vini Jr shines for Brazil, Morocco head to last 32 Scotland vs Brazil Highlights, FIFA World Cup 2026: Neymar's return rounded off a perfect day for Brazil as they made it to the round of 32 with a 3-0 win. Morocco overcame a scare from Haiti to make it to the next round.

Ben Stokes apologises to England teammates, urges focus on New Zealand series decider ENG vs NZ 3rd Test: Ben Stokes said he apologised to England teammates for the distraction caused by his curfew-breach controversy and urged the squad to focus on the series-deciding third Test against New Zealand at Trent Bridge.

Shafali Verma reveals the work behind her growing impact with the ballWomen's T20 World Cup, IND vs BAN: India opener Shafali Verma backed the team's ability to handle pressure, insisting the focus remains on Bangladesh despite a must-win situation. The all-rounder also embraced her growing bowling role, saying she now sees herself as an all-rounder.

FIFA WC Highlights: Switzerland, Canada head to knockouts while Bosnia waitFIFA World Cup 2026, Canada vs Switzerland, Bosnia vs Qatar Highlights: Switzerland secure a 2-1 win over Canada but both teams qualify for the Round of 32. Bosnia keep their hopes alive with a 3-1 victory against Qatar.

India's 2036 Olympics bid enters key phase as IOC revamps host selection processIndia's bid to host the 2036 Olympics received a clearer roadmap after the IOC approved a new Strategic Dialogue phase in the selection process. The host city will now be decided in mid-2029, with Ahmedabad among the leading contenders.

Dev Meena, Anushka Yadav shine on Inter-State C'ships, seal Asiad 2026 spotsAsian Games qualification took centre stage on Day 1 of the National Inter-State Championships, with multiple athletes achieving the required standards and Gulveer Singh claiming gold in the men's 5000m.

Worked hard on my bowling: Ind opener Shafali Verma on her growing all-round roleWomen's T20 World Cup 2026, IND vs BAN: Shafali Verma said preparation and visualisation have sharpened her value as India's part-time bowling option before the Bangladesh game. Her added role gives India balance as Harmanpreet Kaur's side confront a must-win stretch after the South Africa defeat.

No additional revenue for FIFA: President Infantino justifies hydration breaksFIFA World Cup 2026: Gianni Infantino has defended FIFA's World Cup hydration breaks against claims of an advertising grab. The row matters because player welfare, tactical interference and commercial suspicion now sit in the same stoppage.

Indian fast bowler Akash Deep Singh marries in VaranasiIndia pacer Akash Deep will marry Akshita Raj in Varanasi after days of ceremonies in Bihar. Viral clips from his tilak and a festive Baddi have pushed the celebration well beyond the family courtyard.

Vaibhav Sooryavanshi reacts to brother's impressive knock: Hundreds run in bloodAshirvad Sooryavanshi made 168 off 119 balls in a local match, with Vaibhav sharing the scorecard online. The innings deepened talk of another Sooryavanshi batting talent emerging from Bihar.

Amid terrible batting form, Harmanpreet Kaur finds advice from ex-teammateIndia captain Harmanpreet Kaur's batting form has become a growing concern at the Women's T20 World Cup, but former teammate Shikha Pandey believes the solution lies in returning to her natural game.

Too young for dressing room? Sooryavanshi's UK tour comes with a twistVaibhav Sooryavanshi will use separate changing facilities during India's England tour under ICC safeguarding rules. The arrangement shows how cricket's child-protection systems are adapting to a 15-year-old entering the senior game.

Chasing like Dhoni Book Review: Story of the other IndiaAyush Purthan and Samod's Chasing Like Dhoni examines India's grassroots cricket world through the shift triggered by MS Dhoni's rise. The book shows how that dream widened beyond big cities while exposing its emotional, financial and structural costs.

Shubman Gill surpasses Kohli in ODI rankings, Root reigns supreme in TestsIndia ODI captain Shubman Gill has climbed to the second spot in the ODI batting rankings. Meanwhile, Joe Root, who became only the 2nd batter to reach 14,000 Test runs, has regained his place as the No.1 Test batter in the world.

Divided by fans, united by stats: Two GOATS, uncanny similaritiesFIFA World Cup 2026: Ronaldo silenced retirement calls with a record-setting brace as Portugal beat Uzbekistan 5-0. His reply dragged Messi back into frame, with both greats now sharing an identical 20-year, 11-day World Cup scoring span.

Vaibhav Sooryavanshi reminds Kapil Dev of 16-year-old Sachin TendulkarIndia's first World Cup-winning captain, Kapil Dev has compared Vaibhav Sooryavanshi to a young Sachin Tendulkar, but said it is far too early to judge the teenager's career.

Axed from Asian Games, Manika Batra threatens to sue table tennis associationOne of India's top table tennis players, Manika Batra, has threatened legal action against the Table Tennis Federation of India (TTFI). Batra, who was left out of India's Asian Games squad, has demanded transparency from the federation over the selection process.

Stokes is a good friend: McCullum dismisses talk of fallout with England captainEngland coach Brendon McCullum dismissed speculation of a fallout with captain Ben Stokes, insisting the pair remain "good friends" and have "no idea" where the rumours originated.

Ghana witch doctor strikes? Harry Kane fails to score in England's World Cup drawGhanaian fans have been celebrating the role of self-styled witch doctor Nana Kwaku Bonsam after Harry Kane endured a frustrating evening in England's goalless draw against Ghana at the FIFA World Cup 2026.

Congo superfan finally arrives in USA, aura farms at FIFA World CupDR Congo super fans Michel Kuka Mboladinga finally arrived at the FIFA World Cup 2026 on Wednesday, June 24. Standing tall in the team's match against Colombia, Mboladinga was a source of inspiration for the team.

Daniel Munoz sends Colombia into knockouts with narrow win over DR CongoDaniel Munoz's second-half goal finally broke DR Congo's resistance as Colombia secured a hard-fought 1-0 victory in Guadalajara on Tuesday to book a place in the knockout stage of the FIFA World Cup 2026.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
